title: calamintha officinalis var. nepeta
intro: Calamintha officinalis var. nepeta, also known as Nepeta officinalis or Calamint, is a perennial herb in the mint family (Lamiaceae) characterized by its aromatic leaves and small blue flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and carminative properties. The health benefits of Calamintha officinalis var. nepeta include soothing digestive issues, reducing anxiety and stress, and alleviating headaches and fever. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and respiratory problems.
